preamble: The palatalized voiced bilabial nasal /mʲ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the palatal nasal /ɲ/. This shift typically involves the strengthening of the palatalization feature, leading to a complete change in the place of articulation from bilabial to palatal, while maintaining the nasal manner of articulation.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, Place Assimilation (from bilabial to palatal), Fortition (of the palatal gesture)

examples.note: Instances of /ɲ/ in Yawalapiti that alternate with /m/ are believed to derive from a palatal allophone of Pre-Yawalapiti *m, which would have been realized as [mʲ] in palatalizing environments.

sources: P. (2014). Internal and Comparative Reconstruction in Yawalapiti: Palatalization and Rule Telescoping. International Journal of American Linguistics, 80(3), 291-320.
